blocks.content: They stayed up there longer than they should have. Nobody was in a hurry to get back to the ground.
Coming down was harder than going up, the way it always is. You trust the rungs backward, your feet probing for each one.
Kit went easy. Eli went with a lot of commentary. Savannah went methodically. Daisy missed one rung but recovered so fast it almost didn't happen.
The ground felt different after sixty feet — solid in a way you didn't notice until you'd been away from it.
They got their bikes. The heat had settled from something pleasant into something serious, even the shade between houses feeling warmer than an hour ago.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: That's a structural fact about me, yes.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We're not inviting ourselves. We're arriving. There's a difference.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: My mama tolerates you. That's different too.
But she was already turning her bike toward her street.
